overview: Rakdos Monument leverages Lorwyn Eclipsed additions to return Monument to Endurance to its aggressive roots. By using Iron-Shield Elf and other free discard sources, the deck consistently triggers the artifact for value while establishing board presence with hard-to-remove creatures. It wins through a combination of beatdown, recurring graveyard threats, and Monument's reach and card advantage.
win_conditions: Classic beatdown paired with strong removal
Recurring threats discarded from the graveyard
Monument to Endurance triggers providing reach and card advantage

keep_criteria: Hands with early discard outlets or lands to trigger Monument to Endurance
Hands with low-cost creatures to establish early board presence
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, a turn-1 or turn-2 discard/removal spell, and at least one early threat or mana accelerator.
Prioritize hands that can play a land, cast a cheap interaction, and develop toward the Monument by turn 3.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early interaction or discard sources
Hands with only high-cost permanents without early triggers
Mulligan any hand with 0-1 lands, multiple expensive cards with no early play, or only removal without threats or mana fixing.

examples.reason: Provides early land, discard outlet, and artifact trigger potential consistent with the aggressive game plan.

early_game: Play Monument to Endurance and lands to establish the engine
Use early discard outlets like Iron-Shield Elf or Restless Vents to trigger Monument
Deploy hard-to-remove creatures to apply early pressure
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cheap discard or early threat if available.
Turn 2: Develop mana, drop the Monument if you have the mana, or establish a midrange threat.
Prioritize board presence and card advantage over rushing
the Monument needs time to grow and stabilize against the fast metagame.
mid_game: Cycle through discard sources to maintain Monument triggers and card advantage
Use Moonshadow to fetch high-power creatures like Flamewake Phoenix
Leverage Bloodthorn Flail and Bloodghast to return discarded threats to the battlefield
Balance mana for interaction versus Monument development.
Avoid overcommitting to the artifact early
keep mana open for disruption and protection.
Manage your graveyard carefully if the deck uses it for recursion or value, and prioritize drawing into late-game threats when the board is stabilized.
Commit threats when you have removal or discard to clear blockers or answer sweepers.
Hold back if the opponent has heavy removal or counterspells and you lack protection.
late_game: Cycle Marauding Mako for card advantage rather than playing it as a one-drop
Sideboard in attrition tools like Case of the Crimson Pulse and Sunspine Lynx
Use sweepers like Pyroclasm to clear wide boards while protecting recurring threats
A fully grown Monument-boosted creature, often paired with direct damage or a resilient threat like Moonshadow.
Sometimes a sacrifice outlet combined with recursion or evasion can close games.
Direct damage spells can also finish games if the opponent is low and your board is established.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, the opponent is low on removal, or you've established board control.
Commit aggressively if the Monument is untouchable or if you can trade efficiently into their blockers.
Push damage when you know they lack sweepers or counters.

key_cards.name: Iron-Shield Elf
key_cards.notes: Lorwyn Eclipsed addition that consistently triggers Monument to Endurance.

key_cards.name: Monument to Endurance
key_cards.notes: Priority trigger order is Draw, Damage, Treasure unless a specific trigger wins or extra mana is needed.

key_cards.name: Moonshadow
key_cards.notes: Reliable staple to fetch permanents; ideally used with power four or greater to survive combat and trigger Flamewake Phoenix.

key_cards.name: Flamewake Phoenix
key_cards.notes: Key payoff for discard outlets; provides immediate board impact and positive value when cycled.

key_cards.name: Bloodthorn Flail
key_cards.notes: Recurring discard source that helps reach the four-power threshold for Flamewake Phoenix and can return Bloodghast.

key_cards.name: Tersa Lightshatter
key_cards.notes: Provides two discards from a single effect with immediate battlefield impact; has a spell-reuse ability.

key_cards.name: Bloodghast
key_cards.notes: Returns from graveyard when discarded or destroyed via Landfall; enables same-turn Flamewake Phoenix returns.

key_cards.name: Inti, Seneschal of the Sun
key_cards.notes: Generates card advantage on discards but does not draw cards, so it doesn't fuel discard outlets.

key_cards.name: Marauding Mako
key_cards.notes: Payoff for discards; cycling it away is often more reliable late-game than playing it as a one-drop.

key_cards.name: Bitter Triumph
key_cards.notes: Contributes to discard count while unconditionally handling creatures and Planeswalkers.

key_cards.name: Requiting Hex
key_cards.notes: New Lorwyn Eclipsed card that eases interaction against Badgermole Cub, Dimir Midrange, and Mono Red Aggro.

key_cards.name: Restless Vents
key_cards.notes: Functions as both a threat and a discard source for Monument to Endurance.

key_cards.name: Sunspine Lynx
key_cards.notes: Exploits greedy shock land mana bases in longer games; watch out for own non-basic count.

key_cards.name: Case of the Crimson Pulse
key_cards.notes: Fuels Monument to Endurance and provides extra resources each turn to maintain attrition wars.

key_cards.name: Duress
key_cards.notes: Classic staple to delay opponent's game plan or ensure key cards resolve unimpeded.

key_cards.name: Soul-Guide Lantern
key_cards.notes: Necessary against Sultai Reanimator and Superior Spider-Man/Doomsday Excruciator combos; strong answer against Lessons.

key_cards.name: Pyroclasm
key_cards.notes: Clears wide boards early; leverages indestructible, power growth, and graveyard recursion to our advantage.

key_cards.name: Shoot the Sheriff
key_cards.notes: Rounds out spot removal for specific threats outside Requiting Hex's range.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize Monument to Endurance triggers in the order of Draw, Damage, Treasure unless a specific trigger wins immediately or extra mana is needed for an explosive play.
Use Moonshadow to fetch creatures with power four or greater to survive combat and trigger Flamewake Phoenix.
Combine Bloodthorn Flail with Bloodghast to return Flamewake Phoenix to the battlefield on the same turn for board consistency.
Cycle Marauding Mako in the late game rather than playing it as a one-drop that does nothing on its own.
Common mistake: Overcommitting to the Monument too early and getting wiped by sweepers or targeted removal.
Another pitfall: Ignoring the graveyard if the deck uses it for recursion or value.
Unique interaction: Moonshadow likely synergizes with the Monument through recursion, evasion, or sacrifice outlets.
Always track opponent's mana and removal to time your key plays, and don't be afraid to play around sweepers by holding up interaction or attacking in waves.

matchups.advice: Sideboard in Requiting Hex and interaction to handle their game plan.
Sideboard in Soul-Guide Lantern as a strong answer against Lessons.
Use Pyroclasm to clear go-wide strategies like Nature's Rhythm.
Sideboard in Pyroclasm and Requiting Hex to handle early pressure and wide boards.
Sideboard in Soul-Guide Lantern to counter Superior Spider-Man and Doomsday Excruciator combos.
Focus on attrition and recurring threats to outlast their resources.
Leverage Monument triggers and removal to handle their flying threats.

Use cheap, uncounterable threats or discard to remove counterspells before they can be cast. Cast key spells at instant speed when the opponent is tapped out. Rely on the Monument as a secondary win condition that doesn't require casting spells, and use discard to disrupt their hand before they can answer your threats.

Apply early pressure to disrupt their mana development and use discard to hit key ramp pieces. Stabilize with sweepers and early removal, then close out the game before they hit their late-game power. Don't let them stabilize; trade efficiently and pressure their life total.

Prioritize early removal and discard to slow their board development. Don't overextend; the Monument wins long games, so survive turns 1-3, stabilize, and out-value them. Use direct damage to trade down favorably and protect your life total while developing your board.

Pressure their life total with direct damage and resilient threats. Force them to use removal on the Monument, then exploit the card advantage. Use discard to disrupt their game plan and force unfavorable trades. Don't let them stabilize; keep applying pressure and win the resource war.

Win the resource war by prioritizing card advantage, protecting your Monument, and forcing unfavorable trades. Discard their key threats and out-value them in the late game. Track their removal and counters, and play around their sideboard by keeping a balanced hand of interaction and threats.

common_threats: Watch for Izzet's counterspells—hold up mana for protection or cast key spells at instant speed when they're tapped out.
Track Landfall triggers and wide boards

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Rakdos Monument leverages the additions from Lorwyn Eclipsed to bring Monument to Endurance back to the aggressive roots... using free discard sources to generate value repeatedly each turn cycle while establishing board presence with hard-to-remove creatures.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: This approach gives you three distinct paths to victory: classic beatdown paired with strong removal, recurring threats you discard from the graveyard, and Monument to Endurance triggers providing reach and card advantage.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: The priority, in most cases, will be Draw, Damage, Treasure unless a specific trigger can win the game immediately, or if we need the extra mana for a more explosive play.
We typically want Moonshadow with power four or greater—ideal for surviving most combat while also triggering Flamewake Phoenix
We can use Bloodthorn Flail with it to return Flamewake Phoenix to the battlefield on the same turn, giving us more consistency in keeping creatures on board.
there are scenarios—especially in the late-game — where Cycling it away is a more reliable option than putting a one-drop on the board that does nothing on its own.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: makes interaction much easier against Badgermole Cub decks, Dimir Midrange, and Mono Red Aggro.
now that players are using Superior Spider-Man with Doomsday Excruciator and other powerful cards.
from Badgermole Cub / Nature's Rhythm lists to Mono Red Aggro.
